RepresentativeInfo.CapitolAddress: MO House of Representatives, 201 West Capitol Avenue, Room 101-G, Jefferson City MO 65101
RepresentativeInfo.Biography:
Representative Kenneth Jamison, a Democrat, proudly represents part of Clay County (District 15) in the Missouri House of Representatives. He was elected to his first two-year term in November 2024.
Rep. Jamison was raised and resides in District 15. He attended Meadowbrook Elementary, Antioch Middle, and Oak Park High schools.
A United States Marine Corps veteran, Rep. Jamison brings a strong sense of discipline and service to his work in the legislature. He earned his Bachelor of Arts degree from the University of Missouri in 2008 and later received his Juris Doctorate in 2016, demonstrating his commitment to education and legal expertise.
In addition to his legislative duties, Rep. Jamison is a practicing attorney, applying his legal background to advocate for his community. Outside of work, he enjoys spending quality time with his wife, Sierra, their son, Benjamin, and two dogs, Buddy and Maple Waffles, cherishing the values of family and service that guide both his personal and professional life.

SponsoredBill.LongTitle: Requires peace officers to be legally authorized to work in the United States under federal law
Requires long-term care facilities to maintain liability insurance
Creates the "First Responder Tuition Assistance Act" to provide free college tuition for first responders and their spouse and descendants

CoSponsoredBill.LongTitle: Creates provisions relating to compensation for assisting with veterans benefits matters
Designates the last week in April each year as "Infertility Awareness Week"
Proposes a constitutional amendment relating to property tax exemptions
